message:
chore(beads): close br-qiuzo (fixed in ddb92dcc)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1980,7 +1980,7 @@
 {"id":"br-q0e0u","title":"Fix am robot subcommands rejecting canonically registered agents as agent not found","description":"Observed on /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust after registering CoralMarsh via canonical Agent Mail tools (macro_start_session / register_agent). Canonical MCP tools can resolve and use the identity: fetch_inbox(project_key, agent_name=CoralMarsh) succeeds, send_message from CoralMarsh succeeds, and file reservations for CoralMarsh succeed. But several CLI robot subcommands reject the same identity with: error: invalid argument: agent not found: CoralMarsh. Repros: AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli am robot status --project /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust --agent CoralMarsh --format json; AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli am robot inbox --project /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust --agent CoralMarsh --all --format json; AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli am robot reservations --project /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust --agent CoralMarsh --all --format json. Historical agent MistyRiver also reproduces. Notably, AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli am robot agents --project /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust --agent CoralMarsh --format json succeeds, so this appears to be subcommand-specific agent resolution/validation divergence inside the robot CLI path rather than missing project data. Investigate the robot read model and agent validation flow, make canonical tool-registered agents visible consistently across robot subcommands, and add regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GrayRidge","owner":"CoralMarsh","created_at":"2026-03-13T21:32:14.221354077Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T09:38:44Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T09:38:44Z","close_reason":"Fixed in da7e2f17: resolve_robot_scope now retries local DB when archive snapshot misses agent. Added is_agent_not_found_error helper + 2 regression tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1228,"issue_id":"br-q0e0u","author":"CoralMarsh","text":"Implemented source-side fix for robot agent resolution divergence: when explicit project/agent scope is missing in the local robot SQLite read model but present in the canonical Git archive, robot now reconstructs an archive-backed temp snapshot and retries resolution there. Also extended archive reconstruction to ingest file_reservations so reservations-dependent robot views can rebuild from canonical artifacts. Added regressions for missing-agent archive fallback and reservation reconstruction dedupe. Verification: rustfmt on touched files passed; rch cargo fmt --check passed; rch cargo check/clippy blocked by unrelated upstream Frankensearch compile errors after LockError::PolledAfterCompletion was added in asupersync (for example frankensearch-rerank/src/lib.rs:478 and frankensearch-embed/src/fastembed_embedder.rs:465).","created_at":"2026-03-13T22:03:40Z"},{"id":1230,"issue_id":"br-q0e0u","author":"CoralMarsh","text":"Follow-up verification detail on 2026-03-13: I also tried narrowing remote verification to the database crate to validate reconstruct.rs independently of the full workspace. Results via rch: (1) cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-db --no-default-features --lib --tests fails due pre-existing feature-gating problems in search_v3/query_assistance/search_response when tantivy-engine symbols are compiled out, and (2) cargo check / cargo test with --no-default-features --features tantivy-engine still fail before reaching the new reconstruct test because mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_service.rs currently references env_u64 in a configuration where it is not in scope (search_service.rs:2336 and :2342). These blockers are outside the touched robot.rs / reconstruct.rs paths, so current verification status remains: formatting passed, but compile/test verification is blocked by pre-existing unrelated search-layer breakage.
","created_at":"2026-03-13T22:11:22Z"},{"id":1234,"issue_id":"br-q0e0u","author":"CoralMarsh","text":"Fresh-eyes follow-up after the initial robot fallback patch:
- fixed archive fallback agent-profile detection to be case-insensitive when scanning archived `agents/{name}/profile.json` directories
- fixed a regression where `am robot agents` unnecessarily validated `--agent` through the new fallback scope path even though that subcommand only needs project resolution
- tightened the archive fallback regression to exercise a lowercase explicit agent name against a mixed-case archived profile directory

Verification status remains limited by unrelated workspace breakage. The latest targeted `rch` run for `cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-cli resolve_robot_scope_falls_back_to_archive_snapshot_for_missing_explicit_agent -- --nocapture` did not fail in the robot patch itself; it was blocked again upstream when the CLI crate pulled in Frankensearch and hit the existing `LockError::PolledAfterCompletion` non-exhaustive match in `frankensearch-embed/src/fastembed_embedder.rs:465`.
","created_at":"2026-03-13T22:43:54Z"},{"id":1235,"issue_id":"br-q0e0u","author":"CoralMarsh","text":"Fresh-eyes pass found and fixed an additional reconstruct.rs bug: archive reconstruction now accepts legacy/Python file-reservation artifacts that store the reservation path under 'path' instead of only 'path_pattern'. Expanded the reservation reconstruction test to cover both mirrored Rust-format and Python-format artifacts. rustfmt passed. rch-targeted cargo verification is still blocked before test execution by unrelated existing issues: upstream frankensearch-embed LockError::PolledAfterCompletion on default features, and existing mcp-agent-mail-db feature-gating/search_service env_u64 errors under --no-default-features.","created_at":"2026-03-13T22:55:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-q41fu","title":"Fix dashboard summary panels showing duplicated widgets, inflated latencies, and empty project/reservation data","description":"The Dashboard screen is rendering obviously incorrect operator state: tool latency panels report wildly inflated p95/avg figures for lightweight tools, project and reservation panels render 'No matching' despite active data across many projects, and lower dashboard widgets appear duplicated/repeated in the same frame. Diagnose each pipeline carefully, fix root causes in the dashboard/TUI code, add targeted regressions, and restore accurate live operator situational awareness.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-11T21:19:31.864970957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T09:50:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T09:50:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","metrics","projects","reservations","tui"],"comments":[{"id":1205,"issue_id":"br-q41fu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CoralElk taking a bounded dashboard investigation lane focused on crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/dashboard.rs first. Current repo state suggests br-t2x8z remains blocked upstream in sibling frankensearch, while this dashboard bead has no visible owner comment yet. I am avoiding the already-dirty tui_poller.rs/projects.rs surfaces until I confirm whether the root cause can be fixed in clean dashboard.rs rendering/query-state logic alone.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:33:12Z"},{"id":1209,"issue_id":"br-q41fu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CoralElk dashboard.rs slice landed. Changed the dashboard quick-query scope so live snapshot panels (agents/contacts/projects/project load/reservations) no longer blank themselves based on the free-form query text; query-specific hits remain in the dedicated query panel instead. Also made empty-state copy more truthful: e.g. projects now distinguish 'details unavailable'/'no projects' instead of always 'No matching...', and the same for agents/contacts. Remote verification via rch passed: env TMPDIR=/data CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_coralelk_q41fu_check cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-server --lib --tests. cargo fmt --check --package mcp-agent-mail-server is still blocked by pre-existing formatting drift in crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/startup_checks.rs, which I left untouched because it is outside this reserved lane.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:43:46Z"},{"id":1210,"issue_id":"br-q41fu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Follow-up verification: targeted remote regression test passed with the same /data-backed rch path: env TMPDIR=/data CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_coralelk_q41fu_test cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-server snapshot_panel_query_terms_ignore_dashboard_quick_query -- --nocapture. This confirms the new helper semantics and the dashboard.rs slice compiles/tests cleanly on the server crate path even though full workspace quality gates remain noisy outside this bead.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:49:12Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-q9u9v","title":"perf-verify","status":"tombstone","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-19T00:14:09.507588431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:13:31.384352514Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:13:31.384352514Z","source_repo":".","deleted_at":"2026-04-03T01:13:31.384290809Z","deleted_by":"br-rebuild","delete_reason":"rebuild: not in JSONL","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"br-qiuzo","title":"concurrency: dispatch timeout can hold permit forever when blocking work ignores cancellation","description":"Repro:
1. Run a JSON-RPC dispatch through run_cancellable_blocking_dispatch with dispatch_timeout_secs=1.
2. Move a DispatchPermit into the blocking closure.
3. Make the blocking closure ignore DispatchCancel/Cx and sleep or block for an unbounded duration.
4. Observe the HTTP/dispatch response returns a timeout near 1s, but DISPATCH_INFLIGHT remains occupied until the non-cooperative closure exits.

Why this matters:
Commit 8f8dd5f0 correctly requests cooperative cancellation, but asupersync spawn_blocking cannot hard-cancel a running closure. The production dispatch path only checks the cancellation token before/after router calls unless downstream code cooperates via Cx. A non-cooperative DB/tool path can therefore retain the dispatch permit indefinitely after timeout and exhaust admission capacity.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add an adversarial test where the blocking closure ignores DispatchCancel after timeout.
- Prove bounded graceful degradation: either the permit is released by a hard grace mechanism within <5s, or the request is routed through a bounded worker mechanism that cannot permanently consume all dispatch capacity.
- Preserve timeout response latency near the configured dispatch timeout.
- Document the asupersync spawn_blocking cancellation semantics in the dispatch helper/test so future changes do not assume hard cancellation.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:35:57.053868805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:38:36.693289055Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","dispatch","server"]}
+{"id":"br-qiuzo","title":"concurrency: dispatch timeout can hold permit forever when blocking work ignores cancellation","description":"Repro:
1. Run a JSON-RPC dispatch through run_cancellable_blocking_dispatch with dispatch_timeout_secs=1.
2. Move a DispatchPermit into the blocking closure.
3. Make the blocking closure ignore DispatchCancel/Cx and sleep or block for an unbounded duration.
4. Observe the HTTP/dispatch response returns a timeout near 1s, but DISPATCH_INFLIGHT remains occupied until the non-cooperative closure exits.

Why this matters:
Commit 8f8dd5f0 correctly requests cooperative cancellation, but asupersync spawn_blocking cannot hard-cancel a running closure. The production dispatch path only checks the cancellation token before/after router calls unless downstream code cooperates via Cx. A non-cooperative DB/tool path can therefore retain the dispatch permit indefinitely after timeout and exhaust admission capacity.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add an adversarial test where the blocking closure ignores DispatchCancel after timeout.
- Prove bounded graceful degradation: either the permit is released by a hard grace mechanism within <5s, or the request is routed through a bounded worker mechanism that cannot permanently consume all dispatch capacity.
- Preserve timeout response latency near the configured dispatch timeout.
- Document the asupersync spawn_blocking cancellation semantics in the dispatch helper/test so future changes do not assume hard cancellation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:35:57.053868805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:46:57.451151080Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:46:57.450703351Z","close_reason":"Fixed in ddb92dcc","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","dispatch","server"]}
 {"id":"br-ql6bf","title":"Optimize agent-name normalization hot path","description":"Profile-driven optimization of is_valid_agent_name/normalize_agent_name to improve register_agent latency without changing accepted names or output normalization.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:35:24.334100447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-11T04:53:54.026627970Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T04:53:54.021005552Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-qni29","title":"T3.2: Email, broadcast, and descriptive name detection parity","description":"Ensure categories 3 (EMAIL_AS_AGENT), 4 (BROADCAST_ATTEMPT), and 5 (DESCRIPTIVE_NAME) detect exactly the same inputs with identical error messages. EMAIL: '@' in value and '.' in value.split('@')[-1]. BROADCAST: value.lower().strip() in {all, everyone, broadcast, *}. DESCRIPTIVE: contains dev/developer/agent/worker/assistant/helper/bot/admin/user/manager/lead/engineer. All keyword sets and messages must match character-for-character.","notes":"Implemented category-3/4/5 agent-name mistake parity in crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/models.rs. Added BROADCAST_TOKENS and DESCRIPTIVE_NAME_KEYWORDS, plus detectors: looks_like_email(), looks_like_broadcast(), looks_like_descriptive_name(). Extended detect_agent_name_mistake() order to include EMAIL_AS_AGENT, BROADCAST_ATTEMPT, DESCRIPTIVE_NAME with exact message text parity strings. Added tests: email_detection_matches_python_rule, broadcast_detection_exact_tokens, descriptive_name_detection_keyword_contains, detect_mistake_email, detect_mistake_broadcast, detect_mistake_descriptive_name. Validation via rch: cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-core mistake -- --nocapture (pass), cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-core (pass), cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-tools (pass).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverFox","created_at":"2026-02-15T02:04:26.674887294Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T03:40:38.391039252Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T03:40:38.368573831Z","close_reason":"Completed EMAIL/BROADCAST/DESCRIPTIVE agent-name detection parity + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doc-parity"]}
 {"id":"br-qzaeq","title":"T7.2: Project/Agent/Inbox resource description parity","description":"Match the exact Python descriptions for project, agent, inbox, and view resources.

resource://projects: "List all projects known to the server in creation order. When to use: Discover available projects when a user provides only an agent name. Build UIs that let operators switch context between projects. Returns: list[dict] Each: { id, slug, human_key, created_at }"

resource://project/{slug}: "Fetch a project and its agents by project slug or human key. When to use: Populate an LDAP-like directory for agents in tooling UIs. Determine available agent identities and their metadata before addressing mail."

resource://agents/{project_key}: Full description including "When to use", "Notes" about agent names NOT being program/user names, resource://agents hints.

resource://inbox/{agent}: Full description with since_ts, urgent_only, include_bodies, limit parameters.

resource://views/urgent-unread/{agent}, views/ack-required/{agent}, views/acks-stale/{agent}, views/ack-overdue/{agent}: Each with their specific descriptions and parameter docs.","notes":"Updated projects, project/{slug}, agents/{project_key}, inbox/{agent} descriptions to match Python exactly. agents/{project_key} now has full 'When to use', 'Parameters', 'Returns', 'Example', 'Notes' sections matching Python docstring.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-15T01:59:52.242968714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T05:02:18.359277798Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T05:02:18.359204140Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doc-parity"]}
